The deception also proves challenging at home, as her children need her support more than ever. She faces a media storm at work, as she advises Fi on her separation from her husband and begins divorce proceedings for the wife of a romantic novelist. Rose wrestles with the emotional impact of her miscarriage, Nina comes to terms with her unplanned pregnancy, and Ruth reconnects with a face from her past. Guest starring Frances Barber(n)

programme.title: Plastic Surgery Undressed
programme.desc: By the age of 30, up to a third of those with male pattern baldness will be losing their hair - and for some it has a devastating impact on their confidence. But is a hair transplant the solution? Here four surgery seekers get a unique opportunity to decide if it is right for them by watching a procedure live, talking to experts and meeting former patients(n)

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern help members of the Humberside Police force combat their sedentary behaviour as they challenge them to bring more movement into their daily routines, fitting them with movement trackers to check their progress. Meanwhile, Hugh tests one of the most popular health crazes to hit the market, CBD, on three volunteers who suffer from insomnia, and Steph meets a family struggling with issues surrounding their body image(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Keepers are taken by surprise with the unexpected birth of a sea lion pup, while one of the park's best known giraffes has become very sick, and vets cannot work out why(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martell Maxwell follow the progress as couples and a father-and-son team tackle renovation projects in Stoke, Liverpool and Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with the Bone family, who spent a trial week sampling life in Australia, to see if they decided to make a permanent move down under or stay put in Scotland(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The enforcement officers crack down on restaurants putting customers' lives at risk and put the brakes on a fake website fleecing motorists out of millions of pounds(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is at Newark Antiques Fair, with experts Tim Weeks and Kate Bliss. Anita also shows off a collection of antiques that are perfect for those with a sweet tooth(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: The tension between Al and Daniel continues to build. Meanwhile, Emma tries to calm an angry patient who is nursing old wounds(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank Hathaway and Luella Shakespeare investigate the disappearance of an Eastern European oligarch. Comedy drama starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Hampshire with a dynamic couple who love the outdoors and have £650,000 to buy a property and, while in the county, visits the Solent Sky Museum(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Northern Ireland's first charcuterie maker, Alistair, is joined by Kenny and Jennifer, as they aim to sell their chorizo, sausages and meat at the Irish Game Fair(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Charles Hanson are in Northumberland and Tyne and Wear, where Margie finds a brass antique but she is baffled as to what it could be, and in a pub in Gateshead, Charles is advised how best to use a sword when dancing(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Liberal Democrats(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team helps project manager Ben in Avening in the Cotswolds, whose life was changed in a split second by a freak accident while holidaying in Cyprus. Ben slipped and fell head first into the shallow end of a pool, breaking his neck in three places, and for him to move back home from Salisbury Spinal Unit, it needs to be adapted with a front access and a two-storey extension with through-floor lift, along with all the bespoke care provision that he will need, including a home-office space(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, the spy cameras travel to the Arctic and Antarctic circles, following chicks in a king penguin colony on the island of South Georgia in the Southern Ocean. On the other side of the world, Spy Walrus discovers a walrus family hauled out on an ice floe, before Spy Gyrfalcon follows the family as they head towards dry land. Elsewhere on Norway's Hornoya island, Spy Puffin follows thousands of male puffins as they gather in the snow and search for their life-long partners(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Freddie has unanswered questions for his ex-Zak, and hopes that Zak can be honest about why they split. Holly tries to work out why Ben dumped her after she met his family(n)

programme.title: My Mate's a Bad Date
programme.desc: Oloni has his work cut out with Vicky as he helps her find her perfect match, hoping to change her behaviour and her unrealistic criteria for her suitors(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Derry-born Ciara McEleney wanted to move to Perth to give her daughters a sunshine lifestyle, but husband Ruairi had big concerns. One year on Nicki Chapman returns to find out what they decided to do(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the City Hall in Kingston-upon-Hull, antiques experts Caroline Hawley and Nick Hall hunt for objects to take to auction. Presenter Paul Martin heads to Sewerby Hall in Bridlington to learn the life story of Hull girl Amy Johnson, the first woman to fly solo from England to Australia(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the return of the competition in which 10 home cooks move into a shared house and compete in a series of weekly challenges designed to demonstrate their flair, skill and creativity. There is also a new addition to the judging line-up as Michelin-starred chef Angela Hartnett joins home cooking queen Mary Berry and produce expert Chris Bavin. For their first two challenges, the cooks have to come up with their ultimate pasta dinner and a dish based on a piece of fruit(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Walks of Life
programme.desc: Mehreen Baig follows in the footsteps of Charles Dickens as she explores the Kent countryside, visiting the setting of Great Expectations and the house where the author died(n)

programme.title: Super Cute Animals
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan embarks on a global journey to find some of the world's cutest animals, aiming to understand why people find them so endearing and revealing the surprising science behind each creature. He begins with the giant panda, whose unusually large head tells a story of millions of years of evolution and survival. Gordon also reveals how the fennec fox's ears are crucial to its survival, discovers the surprising secret behind a penguin's waddle and meets a five-year-old chimpanzee whose giggle reveals new information about man's evolution(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: With Sharon out of action, Robbie's fate is sealed. Niamh's bad behaviour results in betrayal and the Hennessys are desperate to protect themselves(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: Historians Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn relive the golden age of steam. They begin by exploring how the introduction of steam railways in the early 19th century created a domestic revolution, from the way people lived to the food they ate. Arriving at Ffestiniog Railway in Snowdonia, they find out how millions of tons of slate were moved down the mountain, while underground, Alex experiences the brutal conditions face by miners in the Llechwedd Quarry(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet UK
programme.desc: Steve Brown, Gillian Burke and Chris Packham investigate the health of the UK's waters and look at stories featuring marine wildlife. Steve reveals how ocean plastic rubbish is turned into kayaks used to help clean up the seas, Gillian goes oyster fishing in Cornwall and Chris meets author Philip Hoare, who thinks everyone should take a daily dip in the sea(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Anita Manning value items brought to Blackburn Cathedral, while Paul Martin learns about how mill workers in the town used to be woken by a human alarm clock(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's rail exploration of South-east Asia continues in Malaysia, along the way learning how rubber engineering ensures railway passengers enjoy a smooth ride(n)

programme.title: The Twinstitute
programme.desc: Chris and Xand van Tulleken explore the best methods to learn a new language, putting the two most popular styles of learning to the test. Twins Des and Tina embark on 30 hours of scheduled lessons with a language teacher, while twins Lia and Nick take on the challenge of self-teaching using apps, but without a teacher it will take dedication and self-discipline for our twins, is this just too much to achieve alone? Elsewhere, sets of twins also test the best ways to lower your calorie intake, and uncover the secrets to looking younger(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contenders to brave the black chair, answering questions on the specialist subjects of the Munros of Scotland, the music and members of Steely Dan, the life and works of Adam Smith and the films of Pixar Animation Studios. They then each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell asks the questions as the Forrests take on the Choristers in the second of the quarter-final matches, with a place in the semi-finals at stake. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between groups of four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Nebuchadnezzar, The Fallen Woman, A Masked Ball, and The Troubadour(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the fourth of the quarter-finals, two more teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Confronting Holocaust Denial with David Baddiel
programme.desc: The Holocaust is one of the most documented, witnessed and written about events in history, yet one in six people worldwide either think its scale has been exaggerated or deny that it took place. In this film, comedian David Baddiel explores the multi-faceted nature of Holocaust denial - in both historical and contemporary terms - in an attempt to understand what motivates this phenomenon and why it is on the rise, both in Britain and across the globe(n)

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Trevor and Julia always want to make Christmas really special for their family, but this year money is so tight that things are not going to be easy. As each door of the Advent calendar opens, everyone will have to do their bit to make the festive season happen. The latest instalment in the darkly comic anthology written by and starring The League of Gentlemen and Psychoville's Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with guest appearances by Debbie Rush, Gaby French, Bobby Schofield and Olly Hudson-Croker(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Return of the documentary showing the challenges faced by the NHS, focusing on the work of seven trusts across Liverpool, whose hospitals stretch to north Wales, Cheshire and the Isle of Man. The city's new state-of-the-art hospital remains unfinished, three years after it was due to open, so two of the biggest hospital trusts are planning an ambitious merger. But this means that hundreds of patients and staff have to be transported across the city, while all the operating theatres bar one are closed(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Darren and Emma are preparing for their first-ever cattle show, but first they have to beat seasoned breeders Grania and Simon at their local Launceston Show(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker, Charlotte Smith and Steve Brown head to the Auchlyne Estate near Killin in Perthshire, the first of four visits they will be making over the next year to chart life there throughout the seasons. Plus, Tom Heap goes back to the classroom to see if the next generation of farmers are being given the training they need(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the world-famous Victoria and Albert Museum in London. Only a small amount of the two million items in the V&A's collection are on display to the public. This series gets a glimpse at the treasure trove kept behind closed doors and follows the experts and conservators as they breathe new life into some of these fragile items, such as Pumpie the 100-year-old toy elephant, who needs urgent TLC, and the Christian Dior gowns that get a new lease of life(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Catching up with the Walters family, who spent a trial week sampling life in Perth two years previously, to discover if they chose to stay in the UK or move to Australia(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two more teams hunt for antiques to sell at auction(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and his team help the Newcombe family in Kent, who need to adapt their home to be able to care for their two-year-old son Arlo, who has condition that means his lungs must be constantly supported by breathing apparatus. Trailing breathing tubes, equipment the size of tumble dryers, and no downstairs bathroom have turned their once safe home into a risky space for the family. Nick and the team have amassed a huge group of volunteers for Arlo and the family, as they set to work creating a safe and inspiring space that Arlo and his family can call home(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures from the latest fixtures, including Hull FC v St Helens at KCOM Stadium and Wigan Warriors v Toronto Wolfpack at DW Stadium(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Janie Gall shows Carole some easy-to-follow and bargain-basement ways of making flower arrangements that look like they have cost the earth(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Cameraman Charlie Hamilton James has been captivated by otters for the last 25 years and has filmed them more than anyone else. Now, through the eyes of three river orphaned otters, he sets out to reveal their survival secrets and exactly why he believes these creatures are so special(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Sharon spots an opportunity - but it could be her last. Jim faces up to local yobs, and Ash suspects a widow's suffering has a human cause(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: Historians Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn continue to relive the golden age of steam and how the Victorian railways created modern Britain. They visit Beamish in County Durham, learning how the rail companies first realised there was money to be made in moving people rather than commodities. Then Peter and Alex live the life of navvies, the men who built the thousands of miles of tracks in all weather conditions, and Ruth finds out what impact the new railroads had on the cottage industries. She also learns about the role of the guard, and the trio learn what precautions were necessary for taking a long journey on a train that was yet to include the necessary facilities(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet UK
programme.desc: Steve Brown uncovers the historic secrets of seabirds in Yorkshire, Gillian Burke finds out sea creatures become stressed, and Chris Packham meets a couple adding to shark science(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Ulster Folk and Transport Museum in Belfast, and also visits a family business that builds racing cars. With experts Mark Stacey and Caroline Hawley(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin go toe to toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the growth of Kuala Lumpur, from a swampland tin mine to a major British colony, before becoming a major economic powerhouse in its own right. He makers jewellery at the Royal Selangor pewter factory, samples Malaysia's national dish, and investigates the scandalous behaviour of an infamous British resident general at the turn of the 20th century(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff tackle the Baja 1000t off-road race. On the test track, a showdown is held between a Renault hot hatch, a Porsche sports car and an old Lamborghini. Guests Laurence and Emilia Fox drop in to the studio(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Britain's departure from the EU brings changes for Newlyn, Cornwall's biggest fishing port. The harbourmaster oversees a redevelopment of the fish market, as well as making plans to transform the harbour's ageing infrastructure. However, many fishermen resent his grand plans, feeling that they were not consulted over massive changes to their working environment. While many fishermen supported leaving the EU to gain control over quotas, many now fear that their industry will be sacrificed during negotiations(n)

programme.title: Universal Credit: Inside the Welfare State
programme.desc: The final episode of the series comes from Bolton, a city with unusually high levels of unemployment for people between 18 and 25. Waitress Jenny works on a zero hour contract, and since universal credit is paid a month in arrears, she finds it very difficult to predict how much money she will have to live off at any given time. New claimant Paula is given an advance payment to ease her through the five-week transition period to universal credit, but finds herself struggling to pay it back and live on her monthly payments(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam is stressed by having to prepare for a colonoscopy, and has her last session with therapist Doctor Miller. Matthew Broderick guest stars(n)

programme.title: The Archiveologists
programme.desc: Comedians Diane Morgan and Joe Wilkinson parody a 1950s film about what happens in the bedroom(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Confronting Holocaust Denial with David Baddiel
programme.desc: The Holocaust is one of the most documented, witnessed and written about events in history, yet one in six people worldwide either think its scale has been exaggerated or deny that it took place. In this film, comedian David Baddiel explores the multi-faceted nature of Holocaust denial - in both historical and contemporary terms - in an attempt to understand what motivates this phenomenon and why it is on the rise, both in Britain and across the globe(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: The couples are split up for a navigation challenge that pits men against women on a trek through the wilderness. A local search and rescue expert oversees their efforts and offers his thoughts on which couple should be the next to be eliminated from the contest. After another pair has gone home, the remaining competitors are taken to a gun range to be introduced to the Alaskan attitude to firearms and put their marksmanship to the test(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine the 1927 case of a woman who was drowned, supposedly by a man with a history of impersonating a policeman(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A patient arrives in A&E with mysterious abdominal pains, and Dr Brian Chittick operates on one of his own colleagues, who has two wisdom teeth that need to be removed(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with the Donnelly family who spent a trial week in Melbourne in 2018, and discovers which country they have since chosen to live in(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the City Hall in Kingston-upon-Hull, antiques experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper hunt for objects to take to auction. Caroline is entertained by the story behind some Laurel and Hardy autographs and David is gripped by a collection of postcards between young lovers from the Edwardian era. Presenter Paul Martin talks to playwright James Graham about the legacy of Hull's year as the UK's City of Culture(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Dundee to Aberdeen, stopping off at Glamis Castle, where the Queen Mother grew up, and making an excursion into the Eastern Highlands(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar goes on a ramble that straddles the north and south sides of Calderdale, stopping along the way to sketch and read poetry. She begins in the village of Heptonstall, rambles past Hell Hole and along the canal in Hebden Bridge, before climbing to the Stoodley Pike peace monument(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Walks of Life
programme.desc: JB Gill searches for hidden gems amongst Edinburgh's more familiar tourist sites, and discovers why one of the city's most important religious figures was buried in a car park(n)

programme.title: World's Sneakiest Animals
programme.desc: Naturalist Chris Packham observes the devious tactics and sneaky tricks that some animals carry out in order to survive. New, ground-breaking science helps reveal astonishing visual trickery, from illusionist zebras to vanishing geckos and shape-shifting cuttlefish. Mischievous mimics, from snakes to baby birds disguised as caterpillars, and a lioness who looks like a male lion are also highlighted. The world's ultimate con artists can even rely on sneaky strategies against their own kind, such as the monkey that cheats its family and the lizard that fakes its death(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: A sudden showdown ends in death, after whispered last words. Patricia is prompted to come clean to Johno, and it remains to be seen whether Keith can escape the crime scene(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians reveal how the railways transformed the British diet, rescuing a nation that was struggling to feed itself. Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn discover how the mass transportation of livestock gave birth to the traditional British roast, and Ruth Goodman is in Whitby, which became a thriving fishing town thanks to the North Yorkshire Moors Railway supplying its kippers. Plus, Hampshire's watercress line, which brought nutritional salad to the masses, and how the use of steam-powered engines revolutionised production at Britain's oldest brewery(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet UK
programme.desc: Steve Brown makes a jaw-dropping discovery of sharks' teeth on the beach. Plus, Chris Packham meets a photographer who believes the UK has the best marine life in the world(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Revisiting historic venues from the series, alongside new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin visits the Cotswolds village of Broadway to uncover its artistic past(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Melaka, for centuries one of the greatest trading ports in the world, where he tastes the spicy cuisine of the Kristang community. Continuing to Kluang amid fertile farmland, Michael joins in the pineapple harvest and discovers how Malaysia's resources of tin and tropical fruit created a global market in food. Finally, he arrives at the tip of the Malaysian peninsula in Johor Bahru(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Olympic gold medal-winning swimmer Rebecca Adlington and TV presenter Helen Skelton rummage for items of interest - and value - in the north west of England, assisted by antique experts Roo Irvine and Tim Medhurst. En route to auction in Bolton, they discover the salty secret of Cheshire cheese and hear the story of how, thanks to a Hawaiian Duke, swimming was changed for ever(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: In the final episode, the chef and the group of volunteers discover how much weight they have lost over the past two months, and how much fitter they are as a result of all their hard work. To celebrate their achievements, Tom decides to throw a special picnic by the river, and whips up a series of exciting, healthy recipes they can all enjoy(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: In the final episode, Michael Mosley investigates whether simply thinking about food can help you lose weight, and Giles Yeo finds out how to keep your skin young and healthy. Meanwhile, Alain Gregoire tests whether listening to music improves your sleep, and Guddi Singh examines the latest diet in the headlines which claims to have a good impact on the planet(n)

programme.title: Good Omens
programme.desc: As the series reaches its dramatic conclusion, Adam, Crowley and Aziraphale must work together to save the world from certain doom. Will Heaven and Hell punish Crowley and Aziraphale for betraying them if they do? Dramatisation of Terry Pratchett and Neil Gaiman's book, starring Michael Sheen and David Tennant(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Award-winning stand-up comedian Dane Baptiste plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience and then introduces routines by New York comic Desiree Burch and Liverpool funnyman Chris McCausland(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Universal Credit: Inside the Welfare State
programme.desc: The final episode of the series comes from Bolton, a city with unusually high levels of unemployment for people between 18 and 25. Waitress Jenny works on a zero hour contract, and since universal credit is paid a month in arrears, she finds it very difficult to predict how much money she will have to live off at any given time. New claimant Paula is given an advance payment to ease her through the five-week transition period to universal credit, but finds herself struggling to pay it back and live on her monthly payments(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Mediterranean Voyage
programme.desc: The Black Swan makes good headway as it enters Greek waters, but disaster strikes when the mainsail rips and the vessel barely reaches the next port - Corfu. Back on dry land, Francesco enjoys a game of cricket and meets David Bellamy, who points him in the direction of the island's animal life(n)

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the Antipodean island chain, which has been left to its own devices for 80 million years - with surprising consequences. The opening edition features New Zealand's weirdest and most ancient species, including dinosaur-like reptiles, insects that have become giants, and flightless birds that behave like badgers, while the people who settled developed their own unique culture, and all must battle the natural forces that shape the island nation(n)

programme.title: The 80s with Dominic Sandbrook
programme.desc: The historian concludes his review by looking at the last few years of the decade and a society transformed by accelerated change. Not only did the late 1980s see the rise of the yuppie and the economic big bang in the City of London, but shopping habits changed with the growing popularity of German cars, French wine, Italian fashion and Scandinavian interior design. Alongside this transformation was a growing disconnection from the political elite, signified by the growing rave culture and the community charge riots. For Margaret Thatcher, widely seen as the architect of much of this change, things would not end well(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary following four Thai-Danish couples living in a small fishing community on the west coast of Denmark where more than 900 Thai women are married to the local fishermen, exploring the universal questions of love and romance(n)

programme.title: Art of Germany
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon investigates the story of German art in the 20th century, which was affected by Adolf Hitler's propaganda against modern pieces before the Second World War, and by a divided nation after the conflict. He explores the work of Otto Dix, George Grosz and the age of the Bauhaus, plus post-war examples by Georg Baselitz, Hilla Becher and Joseph Beuys, to see how art found a place at the heart of the nation after the global battle(n)

programme.title: Abducted - Elizabeth I's Child Actors
programme.desc: Thirteen year-old Thomas Clifton was walking to school in December 1600 when he was violently kidnapped by men who claimed that they had legal authority to do so from Elizabeth I herself. Katherine Rundell examines the case, finding that the snatching of Thomas had been organised by a theatrical impresario who intended to put him on the stage as part of a company of child actors(n)

programme.title: Rams: Principles of Good Design
programme.desc: A profile of German Dieter Rams, whose work for consumer products manufacturer Braun and furniture company Vitsoe influenced Apple, but who now regrets being a designer(n)

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the Antipodean island chain, which has been left to its own devices for 80 million years - with surprising consequences. The opening edition features New Zealand's weirdest and most ancient species, including dinosaur-like reptiles, insects that have become giants, and flightless birds that behave like badgers, while the people who settled developed their own unique culture, and all must battle the natural forces that shape the island nation(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford investigates the story of the two brilliant but controversial Hunter brothers, who transformed both medicine and art in 18th-century Britain. Their belief that students could only learn anatomy by carrying out dissections created an unprecedented demand for dead bodies - as well as a market for the growing trade of body-snatching from graveyards(n)

programme.title: King Alfred and the Anglo-Saxons
programme.desc: Historian and broadcaster Michael Wood tells the story of King Alfred the Great, arguing that he and his descendants were England's most influential and important rulers. In the first of three programmes, the presenter details a desperate guerrilla war in Somerset, and establishes how Alfred laid the foundations for a single kingdom of 'all the English'. Filmed in locations including Reading and Rome, with contributions by leading scholars(n)

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian examines how key moments in Britain's history have been manipulated and mythologised, beginning with a look at the founding myths of the Reformation. She argues that Henry VIII may have had deeper motives for bringing about a religious and political schism than his desire for a divorce, and in doing so, laid the foundations for our modern constitution and economic power as an empire. She also questions the monarch's supposed belief in Martin Luther's Protestant revolution(n)

programme.title: The Ruth Ellis Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: In July 1955, Ruth Ellis was the last woman to be hanged in Britain for the cold-blooded murder of lover David Blakely. Gillian Pachter takes a forensic look at the police investigation, and soon discovers worrying assumptions and problematic omissions(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary examining the history of capital punishment, including the protocol of public execution and the science of hanging. Archive information reveals the offences that resulted in the gallows and how children as young as seven were hanged(n)

programme.title: Inside the Medieval Mind
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett explores the variation in thought between the Middle Ages and the present day, beginning with the basic cornerstone of the rational mind - knowledge. For hundreds of years, the ability to learn from literature remained almost exclusively in the hands of monastic orders, but this monopoly came under threat with the discovery of such classical works as Aristotle and Thomas Aquinas(n)

programme.title: Jigs & Wigs
programme.desc: Tyrone native and Irish dance fitness coach Angela Mohan puts her army of athletes through the drill in the lead up to the World Championships. Documentary series in which cameras follow dancing advocates in an attempt to illustrate the vibrant and progressive world they inhabit(n)

programme.title: Jigs & Wigs
programme.desc: Belfast born Gavin Doherty is known internationally for his innovative dress designs and teaching techniques. In this programme he shares the secrets of his success and introduces us to prodigy Ethan White from Carrickfergus(n)

programme.title: Me, My Selfie and I with Ryan Gander
programme.desc: The conceptual artist investigates the selfie, discovering its roots go back hundreds of years before smartphones, and examining what technology is doing to the sense of self(n)

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian examines how key moments in Britain's history have been manipulated and mythologised, beginning with a look at the founding myths of the Reformation. She argues that Henry VIII may have had deeper motives for bringing about a religious and political schism than his desire for a divorce, and in doing so, laid the foundations for our modern constitution and economic power as an empire. She also questions the monarch's supposed belief in Martin Luther's Protestant revolution(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford tells the story of how Dr Henry Gray and Dr Henry Carter produced academic textbook Gray's Anatomy - the world's most famous study of the human body. Based on dissections they performed themselves, the accuracy of its descriptions and the stark beauty of its illustrations made it an instant bestseller(n)

programme.title: World's Busiest Railway 2015
programme.desc: The team considers the challenges faced by Mumbai's Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus and ask what the future holds for the city's 5.5million rail commuters. Anita Rani tackles the railway's head of safety on the measures they're taking to reduce the fatality rate of nine people a day, while Dan Snow meets two eyewitnesses to 2008's terror attacks that claimed the lives of 52 people at the station. John Sergeant reveals the heavy human cost of building the line that connects Mumbai to the south and east of India, and Robert Llewellyn fulfills a childhood dream and becomes a train driver for a day(n)

programme.title: A Very British History
programme.desc: Vlogger Shu Lin explores the history of the Chinese community in the UK from the 1950s through to the present, day, revealing how these migrants transformed Britain's bland post-war dining scene. She finds out how an area of Soho became today's vibrant Chinatown and traces how the community has transformed from relying heavily on the restaurant trade, to one achieving outstanding business and educational success(n)

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Davie Milne skippers trawler Faithlie to Norwegian waters to trial a new style of fishing net, and it doesn't take long for first mate Jakey and the rest of the crew to get to grips with the new technology. In the waters off Orkney, Iain Harcus is aiming for high-value white fish on his boat Aalskere, while back in Peterhead a new scheme is under way to provide the vital skills needed to work in the fishing industry(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kicking off a week of episodes for the soap's 35th anniversary, all taking place on the same day. programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A Panorama investigation into the multinational company's rise to corporate superpower, asking whether there is a dark side to the public's love affair with the firm. Former high-level insiders reveal that its customer obsession has led to a huge data-gathering operation, enabling the company to use what it knows about us to shape not only the future of retail, but the workplace and technology too. The programme also hears from senior executives who say the company is a force for good(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes becomes concerned when Dermot announces he wants to ask his childhood friend Buster, a known criminal, to be his best man. programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy and Sharon encourage Ian to make amends with Bobby. But as he is trying to reach out to his son, the teenager is defending his friends Iqra and Habiba against a gang or racists - and when the situation spirals out of control, Bobby is beaten up and rushed to hospital. As Ian sets off in search of revenge, Kathy receives a strange call from Ben and races off to find him - only to be horrified by what she finds(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A reconciliation between Chloe and Cameron seems unlikely after the previous night's events, but nevertheless, he decides to make a bold move to win back her trust. A familiar face returns to Holby to perform surgery on Carole, and Dominic finds a much-needed source of moral support as he waits for the outcome. Jac makes plans for her first unsupervised visit from Emma, but things soon get out of control(n)

programme.title: The Split
programme.desc: Hannah's double life starts to take its toll, especially as Nathan becomes suspicious of her interactions with Christie. The deception also proves challenging at home, as her children need her support more than ever. She faces a media storm at work, as she advises Fi on her separation from her husband and begins divorce proceedings for the wife of a romantic novelist. Rose wrestles with the emotional impact of her miscarriage, Nina comes to terms with her unplanned pregnancy, and Ruth reconnects with a face from her past. Guest starring Frances Barber(n)

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Investigations into social and political issues affecting Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Plastic Surgery Undressed
programme.desc: By the age of 30, up to a third of those with male pattern baldness will be losing their hair - and for some it has a devastating impact on their confidence. But is a hair transplant the solution? Here four surgery seekers get a unique opportunity to decide if it is right for them by watching a procedure live, talking to experts and meeting former patients(n)

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern help members of the Humberside Police force combat their sedentary behaviour as they challenge them to bring more movement into their daily routines, fitting them with movement trackers to check their progress. Meanwhile, Hugh tests one of the most popular health crazes to hit the market, CBD, on three volunteers who suffer from insomnia, and Steph meets a family struggling with issues surrounding their body image(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited

programme.title: Defenders UK

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Northern Ireland's first charcuterie maker, Alistair, is joined by Kenny and Jennifer, as they aim to sell their chorizo, sausages and meat at the Irish Game Fair(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Charles Hanson are in Northumberland and Tyne and Wear, where Margie finds a brass antique but she is baffled as to what it could be, and in a pub in Gateshead, Charles is advised how best to use a sword when dancing(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team helps project manager Ben in Avening in the Cotswolds, whose life was changed in a split second by a freak accident while holidaying in Cyprus. Ben slipped and fell head first into the shallow end of a pool, breaking his neck in three places, and for him to move back home from Salisbury Spinal Unit, it needs to be adapted with a front access and a two-storey extension with through-floor lift, along with all the bespoke care provision that he will need, including a home-office space(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, the spy cameras travel to the Arctic and Antarctic circles, following chicks in a king penguin colony on the island of South Georgia in the Southern Ocean. On the other side of the world, Spy Walrus discovers a walrus family hauled out on an ice floe, before Spy Gyrfalcon follows the family as they head towards dry land. Elsewhere on Norway's Hornoya island, Spy Puffin follows thousands of male puffins as they gather in the snow and search for their life-long partners(n)

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Panzuto was a mafia boss, and a hardened killer. For years he played a key role in Naples' Camorra, but now he has turned state witness and is helping put his former associates behind bars. Dominic Casciani gains exclusive access to an Italian anti-mafia prison to meet Panzuto and hear his story of love, murder and betrayal. It's a tale which took him from the back streets of Naples to a caravan park in Blackpool. We hear from those at the sharp end of the ongoing battle against organised crime in Naples, and from those who believe the tide has finally turned. Above all we hear from the man himself: why did he decide to break with the Camorra, and what does the future hold for him?(n)

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: Adam and Karen's future is thrown into jeopardy by an unexpected offer, and David is in turmoil over his growing feelings for Robyn. While Jenny braces herself for her first mammogram after her treatment for cancer, Pete helps their son Adam realise his dream. John Thomson, James Nesbitt, Fay Ripley, Hermione Norris and Robert Bathurst star in the concluding instalment of the ninth season of Mike Bullen's long-running comedy drama, with guest appearances by Frances Barber and Paul Ritter(n)

programme.title: The BRITs at 40
programme.desc: Jack Whitehall narrates a look back at the history of the awards ceremony, featuring archive footage of some of the most memorable moments, from Oasis and Blur's rivalry to Stormzy's attack on the government, and classic performances by Adele, Rihanna and Drake. The programme also recalls less successful moments, from the infamous 1989 ceremony hosted by Mick Fleetwood and Sam Fox, and Madonna struggles with her cape. Featuring interviews with stars including Robbie Williams, Mel B, Mick Fleetwood, Little Mix, Sam Smith, Ellie Goulding, Sharon Osbourne, Bros, Dua Lipa, Rick Astley and Tom Jones(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Steve and Snot are humiliated when they attend a 'cool kids' party. Intending to get their own back, the pair decide to arranging their own bash for other unpopular teens, changing their destinies, and prompting a visit from their future selves. Meanwhile, Roger dines out with Haley, but takes offense when the waitress compliments Haley's order but not his. Animated comedy, with the voice of Seth MacFarlane(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Langley Falls plans to build an arena football stadium, but the construction requires the felling of an old tree that Stan bizarrely considers to be his father(n)

programme.title: Ibiza Weekender
programme.desc: David books the hotel's first ever hen party. Riva's newly found confidence has Callum looking to rekindle things, but it may be too little too late(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden feels that being a billionaire clouds his interaction with the ladies, so he pretends to be an ordinary guy and immediately finds success with saleswoman Kate. Alan goes along with the masquerade, pretending to be rich, but the power goes to his head(n)

programme.title: Superstore

programme.desc: Walden decides to tell Kate the truth, so he flies to New York to meet her, but his revelation only succeeds in upsetting the fashion designer. Meanwhile, Alan expresses his enthusiasm for musical theatre with a song-and-dance routine(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel receives a shock, while Frank and Ned argue in the Woolpack about Ned's attitude to Zoe. The village committee decide to hold a memorial service for the air crash victims(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A recently released ex-convict who served time for the rape of a local girl becomes the target of a hate campaign - prompting Rob to point the finger of suspicion at the victim's family. The bobby is shocked when old acquaintance DS Rachel Dawson arrives in Aidensfield on the trail of an escaped prisoner, while Peggy accidentally knocks down an old friend with her car. Jonathan Kerrigan stars(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. The family warms to the French way of life, but Charley is unhappy with Mariette's flirting - and after a little Dutch courage, decides to take matters into his own hands. Drama, starring Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Robert decides that Angie, Ida and himself should all go on a diet(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A Russian trawlerman found hiding in a local woman's shed is accused of being a spy by a Home Office official (The Office star Patrick Baladi) - but Walker and Carol discover the government worker has secrets of his own to hide. Peggy turns detective after an old note found among a dead friend's belongings raises questions about her husband's disappearance. With Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay(n)

programme.desc: The day of the Home Farm Classic Car Rally arrives. Rachel's pregnancy is confirmed, Kathy tells Bernard she thinks she is cracking up, and Eric takes revenge on Frank(n)

programme.desc: Zoe asks Chris what his intentions are towards Rachel, who tells him about the pregnancy. Eric tries to reassure Alan about his financial worries(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Wiltons celebrate their sixth wedding anniversary, and Mavis is thrilled when Derek gives her a new budgie. The Websters decide to change Lauren's name to Sophie(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the murder of a prize-winning author on the eve of the Midsomer Literary Festival. The event goes ahead as planned, but a killing occurs during a blackout at a packed reception hosted by the widow of another writer, and it is not long before the detectives uncover corruption, sexual scandal and financial mismanagement. Guest starring Stella Gonet, with John Nettles and John Hopkins(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A Russian trawlerman found hiding in a local woman's shed is accused of being a spy by a Home Office official (The Office star Patrick Baladi) - but Walker and Carol discover the government worker has secrets of his own to hide. Peggy turns detective after an old note found among a dead friend's belongings raises questions about her husband's disappearance. With Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser(n)

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: The detective interrogates a mysterious Dane who has been caught with the dead body of a diamond dealer in his car, and who proves entirely resistant to his questioning. Maigret is convinced his suspect's sister may know something, but soon discovers that everyone in the small rural town where they live is hiding something. Guest starring Tom Wlaschiha (Game of Thrones) and Kevin McNally (the Pirates of the Caribbean movies)(n)

programme.title: Wild Bill
programme.desc: Comedy drama following an American police chief as he moves to the UK and joins the Lincolnshire Police Force. High-flying police officer Bill Hixon arrives in Boston, Lincolnshire to start a new life with his 14-year-old daughter in an effort to escape painful memories in their recent past. However, Bill is not welcomed by his new team, as his deputry Lydia Price makes no secret of her disdain for him, and local journalist Lisa Cranston grills him on his approach. Starring Rob Lowe, Rachael Stirling and Angela Griffin(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Lewis joins Morse on a trip to Australia, but it is no holiday - they are pursuing a former supergrass who holds the key to a mystery back in Oxford. Going deep into the heart of the Outback, Morse soon begins to miss the comforts of home, particularly his regular tipple, although Lewis finds he has more in common with his Aussie brethren than he thought. Crime drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Con O'Neill(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout(n)

programme.desc: Curly starts work at the betting shop and gets taken advantage of by Jack. Deirdre is embarrassed when Samir's friend Omar shows his dislike of her(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barman from rural inn the Maid in Splendour is shot dead as he walks near a ruined cottage in woodlands - and Barnaby's investigation reveals a clash between charming landlord Michael Bannerman, the unofficial squire of Midsomer Worthy, and his abrasive son Stephen, who now runs the pub. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by William Gaunt, Alan Cox, Dave Hill and Frances Tomelty(n)

programme.desc: Peggy begins a frantic search for David's dog Deefer after a pair of youths steal her car from outside the Aidensfield Arms with the hound in the boot. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Coma victim Harriet Farmer finally wakes up a year after she was attacked while trying to elope, but her boyfriend Finn Robson is still missing. Soon after she regains consciousness, members of a chess club are targeted by a killer. Could the assault that left Harriet for dead be linked to moves on a games board? Guest starring Fay Ripley (Cold Feet), with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Wild Bill
programme.desc: When a mysterious stranger wanders into Boston suffering from amnesia, it provides Bill with the opportunity to crack a notorious unsolved case, but he soon comes to realise that the truth is more complicated than it first appears. Meanwhile, Kelsey succeeds in getting Bill to finally engage with his dead wife's memory, and Oleg puts the pressure on Muriel. Comedy drama following an American police chief as he moves to the UK and joins the Lincolnshire Police Force. Starring Rob Lowe, Rachael Stirling and Angela Griffin(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse

programme.desc: Angharad is furious when Bernard receives a bunch of flowers. Frank buys Kim a sports car as a wedding present. Caroline and Eric row about Alan's situation(n)

programme.desc: Curly tries to stop the betting shop customers from smoking. Rita is flattered when Roger asks her out. Tracy is horrified when Deirdre tells her she is marrying Samir(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samir tells Deirdre to give Tracy some time to come round, and the bride-to-be is furious with Ken when he tells her she is only marrying Samir to keep him in the country(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate crime and mayhem in the countryside as they take on a murder case in picturesque Fletcher's Cross. Undertaker Patrick Pennyman is found dead in his funeral parlour and the detectives suspect a possible link to the local spiritualist church. When another body turns up, the pressure to find the culprit redoubles. John Nettles, John Hopkins and Jane Wymark star(n)

Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barrister is killed with a lethal dose of morphine and suspicion falls on ex-convict Grady Felton, who has recently returned to the village where he committed murder years earlier. However, he has a strong alibi and Barnaby thinks he must be working with an accomplice - until Grady himself is targeted in an arson attack. Detective drama, guest starring Sharon Duce, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Wild Bill
programme.desc: Bill launches a PR offensive when a leaked list of potential redundancies turns his team against him. Meanwhile, a routine burglary turns out to be a much bigger case than first anticipated, and Bill finds himself facing a public panic. DNA discovered at the scene of the crime provides the team with a match, but with the culprit missing, they track down his estranged daughter in the search for answers. Elsewhere, Bill's relationship with Mary begins to deepen, and Oleg keeps up the pressure on Muriel(n)

Guest starring Tom Wlaschiha (Game of Thrones) and Kevin McNally (the Pirates of the Caribbean movies)(n)

programme.title: Wild Bill
programme.desc: Comedy drama following an American police chief as he moves to the UK and joins the Lincolnshire Police Force. High-flying police officer Bill Hixon arrives in Boston, Lincolnshire to start a new life with his 14-year-old daughter in an effort to escape painful memories in their recent past. However, Bill is not welcomed by his new team, as his deputry Lydia Price makes no secret of her disdain for him, and local journalist Lisa Cranston grills him on his approach. Starring Rob Lowe, Rachael Stirling and Angela Griffin(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Lewis joins Morse on a trip to Australia, but it is no holiday - they are pursuing a former supergrass who holds the key to a mystery back in Oxford. Going deep into the heart of the Outback, Morse soon begins to miss the comforts of home, particularly his regular tipple, although Lewis finds he has more in common with his Aussie brethren than he thought. Crime drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Con O'Neill(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout(n)

programme.desc: Coma victim Harriet Farmer finally wakes up a year after she was attacked while trying to elope, but her boyfriend Finn Robson is still missing. programme.desc: Samir tells Deirdre to give Tracy some time to come round, and the bride-to-be is furious with Ken when he tells her she is only marrying Samir to keep him in the country(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate crime and mayhem in the countryside as they take on a murder case in picturesque Fletcher's Cross. Undertaker Patrick Pennyman is found dead in his funeral parlour and the detectives suspect a possible link to the local spiritualist church. When another body turns up, the pressure to find the culprit redoubles. John Nettles, John Hopkins and Jane Wymark star(n)

Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barrister is killed with a lethal dose of morphine and suspicion falls on ex-convict Grady Felton, who has recently returned to the village where he committed murder years earlier. However, he has a strong alibi and Barnaby thinks he must be working with an accomplice - until Grady himself is targeted in an arson attack. Detective drama, guest starring Sharon Duce, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Wild Bill
programme.desc: Bill launches a PR offensive when a leaked list of potential redundancies turns his team against him. Meanwhile, a routine burglary turns out to be a much bigger case than first anticipated, and Bill finds himself facing a public panic. DNA discovered at the scene of the crime provides the team with a match, but with the culprit missing, they track down his estranged daughter in the search for answers. Elsewhere, Bill's relationship with Mary begins to deepen, and Oleg keeps up the pressure on Muriel(n)

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: The team is assigned to investigate a series of plutonium robberies. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt(n)

programme.desc: Part two of two. As more new cases of the mystery illness break out on board the luxury liner, Quincy and Dr Carlisle work against the clock to identify the root of the epidemic(n)

programme.desc: Theo has another run-in with bounty hunter Salathiel Harris, who has returned to New York in pursuit of a bail jumper. Guest starring Swoosie Kurtz (Dangerous Liaisons)(n)

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A man dies suddenly of a mysterious fever and Holmes suspects murder, but a malign influence takes its toll on the detective as he delves into the underworld of London's docklands. Starring Jeremy Brett, with Roy Hudd(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry acts as the bodyguard for a Lebanese businessman and diplomat. However, relationships between the two are strained and when their safe house is raided by gunmen, Terry's opinion of his charge reaches a new depth. Dennis Waterman and Kenneth Griffith star(n)

programme.desc: Part two of two. As more new cases of the mystery illness break out on board the luxury liner, Quincy and Dr Carlisle work against the clock to identify the root of the epidemic(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are trained to act as bodyguards to a Middle Eastern president who is preparing to sign an important peace treaty. programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Nina Wadia, Linford Christie, Sarah Harding and Tyger Drew-Honey pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: The Mountain
programme.desc: A look at the busy summer season, when it is all-hands-on-deck as the epic Snowdon Race brings runners from all over the world to the mountain. Farmers Jo and Fiona deal with the frustrations of the season's busy months as unruly pets attack their livestock, while big crowds bring problems for Rob and the mountain rescue team.(n)

programme.title: The Mountain
programme.desc: Snowdon gradually falls silent in the winter months and the residents around the highest peak in England and Wales have it almost to themselves. Railwayman Steff shuts down the summit centre at Hafod Eryri as the season ends, while Alwena also calls temporary time on her Halfwacafe. Plus, wild swimmer Viv goes for a bracing winter plunge and Jo the mountain farmer finally sets foot on the summit of Snowdon for the first time.(n)

programme.title: Apollo 13 (1995)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the ill-fated 1970 Apollo 13 mission to the moon. Crew leader Jim Lovell is faced with a nightmare when the mission suffers an on-board explosion - leaving the team stranded in space with a dwindling oxygen supply while flight controllers and engineers in Houston try to find a way to bring the crew safely back to Earth. With Tom Hanks, Kevin Bacon, Bill Paxton, Ed Harris and Gary Sinise(n)

programme.title: The Mountain
programme.desc: Snowdon gradually falls silent in the winter months and the residents around the highest peak in England and Wales have it almost to themselves. Railwayman Steff shuts down the summit centre at Hafod Eryri as the season ends, while Alwena also calls temporary time on her Halfwacafe. Plus, wild swimmer Viv goes for a bracing winter plunge and Jo the mountain farmer finally sets foot on the summit of Snowdon for the first time.(n)

programme.title: Apollo 13 (1995)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the ill-fated 1970 Apollo 13 mission to the moon. Crew leader Jim Lovell is faced with a nightmare when the mission suffers an on-board explosion - leaving the team stranded in space with a dwindling oxygen supply while flight controllers and engineers in Houston try to find a way to bring the crew safely back to Earth. With Tom Hanks, Kevin Bacon, Bill Paxton, Ed Harris and Gary Sinise(n)

programme.title: Tony Robinson: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: The actor and history buff negotiates northern England's Coast to Coast route, beginning in the Cumbrian village of St Bees, which though small boasts a sizable church. While travelling through the Lake District, Tony discovers the upper crust origins of the Scotch egg, then at the region's most westerly lake, meets a farmer taking a pioneering approach to protect the natural environment. After a spell in the mountains flanking the Honister Pass, Tony digs into the history of slate extraction, before heading to England's most remote youth hostel, nestled deep in the fells(n)

programme.title: Tony Robinson: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: In the upland hills of the Lake District's Borrowdale valley, Tony meets one of the last tenant sheep farmers, who is facing challenging times but is determined to keep working his farm. The actor and history buff also receives an exclusive tour of the only inhabited island on Derwentwater, learns how to cook chutney at Grasmere, gets a rare sighting of a red squirrel, and boards a tourist boat at Ullswater to discover the integral part they played in the industrial heritage of the area(n)

programme.title: Winter Road Rescue
programme.desc: The devastating impact of the Yorkshire floods in 2019, following people on the frontline of the flood response in some of the worst affected communities, like the village of Fishlake. programme.title: At War with the Noise Next Door

programme.desc: Budding chocolatiers compete for the chance to make their own chocolate bar that will be manufactured and sold by one of the biggest chocolate companies in the world. This time, the chocolatiers are challenged to make bite-sized chocolates based on their favourite desserts, hoping to impress judge Ruth Hinks with their creations. In the second challenge, the competitors must create their own version of a celebration variety tin of chocolates. Elsewhere, the Hairy Bikers visit the Quality Street production line in Halifax(n)

programme.title: Inside
programme.desc: Exploring the world's ultra-luxury property market. Meanwhile, in Beverly Hills, Freedom Jacob Cesar is revamping his $10 million pad, decking it out in designer gear in the hope of making a huge profit(n)

programme.title: BSL Zone

programme.title: Buchanan Rides Alone (1958)
programme.desc: A drifter rides into a town dominated by the members of a ruthless family. Western, starring Randolph Scott, Craig Stevens, Manuel Rojas and Barry Kelley(n)

programme.title: A Man Alone (1955)

Western, directed by and starring Ray Milland, with Raymond Burr, Lee Van Cleef, Mary Murphy and Ward Bond(n)

programme.title: The Man in the Iron Mask (1977)

Swashbuckling adventure, based on the classic story by Alexandre Dumas, starring Richard Chamberlain in a dual role, with Patrick McGoohan, Louis Jourdan and Jenny Agutter(n)

programme.title: Sink the Bismarck! (1960)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War drama, charting the mission to track down and eliminate the pride of the German fleet, a fearsome battleship devastating British vessels in the North Atlantic, orchestrated by a British naval officer recovering from his wife's death in an air raid. Starring Kenneth More, Dana Wynter, Laurence Naismith and Karel Stepanek(n)

programme.title: Playing for Keeps (2012)
programme.desc: A retired footballer returns to his home town, hoping to rebuild his relationship with his son. He thinks coaching the boy's team will help them bond and give him the chance to prove he can be a responsible adult - but finds himself fending off the sexual advances of the players' mothers. Comedy, starring Gerard Butler, Jessica Biel, Uma Thurman and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: The Look of Love Interview Special
programme.desc: Steve Coogan and director Michael Winterbottom discuss their new film, a biopic of British porn entrepreneur Paul Raymond(n)

programme.title: Gone Girl (2014)

As his behaviour grows stranger and more evasive, he finds himself accused of her murder. David Fincher's crime thriller based on Gillian Flynn's novel, starring Ben Affleck, Rosamund Pike and Neil Patrick Harris(n)

programme.title: The Look of Love Interview Special
programme.desc: Steve Coogan and director Michael Winterbottom discuss their new film, a biopic of British porn entrepreneur Paul Raymond(n)

programme.title: The Look of Love (2013)

Starring Steve Coogan, Anna Friel and Imogen Poots(n)

programme.title: Carnage Park (2016)
programme.desc: Two criminals take a woman hostage and flee into the backwoods following a disastrous bank robbery. What should have been a safe haven proves a far greater threat than the cops on their trail when they stray into the territory of a former military sniper driven by his experiences of war and who does not take kindly to strangers on his land. Horror, starring Ashley Bell and Pat Healy(n)

programme.title: Support Your Local Gunfighter (1971)

programme.title: Appointment with Danger (1950)

programme.title: The Killing (1956)

Crime caper, starring Jack Hawkins, Nigel Patrick, Richard Attenborough, Roger Livesey and Bryan Forbes(n)

Action thriller, with Mark Wahlberg, Michael Pena and Danny Glover(n)

Drama, starring Colin Firth and Catherine Keener(n)

programme.title: Donovan's Reef (1963)

Comedy drama directed by John Ford, starring John Wayne, Lee Marvin, Jack Warden, Elizabeth Allen and Cesar Romero(n)

Fantasy adventure, with Kristen Stewart, Chris Hemsworth and Charlize Theron(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After the repairs are completed in Amy's flat, she lies to Sheldon by telling him that the work is still ongoing so they can carry on living together for a while longer. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon goes to great lengths to convince Amy that they should have a baby together after they successfully complete a scientific experiment using their DNA samples. Raj is too embarrassed to tell his friends what the new woman in his life does for a living(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: After watching a particular game show together for years, Adam and Emmy are excited to learn that the producers will be holding auditions at their school. Beverly turns to Bill for help when Barry and Murray become convinced that she is a lucky charm for their beleaguered football team(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Desperate circumstances call for desperate measures as Peralta, Captain Holt, Santiago, Jeffords, Diaz and Boyle are forced to ally themselves with some old enemies(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard faces the difficult decision of choosing between his relationship with Priya and his friendship with Penny, while Sheldon is irritated by one of Howard's magic tricks. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is distraught when his online gaming account is hacked, so his friends decide to find out who is responsible. However, they make the disastrous mistake of confronting the culprit in person. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley gives Ben and Danny the time capsules they made at school, which causes everyone to reflect on what they have achieved in their lives so far. Meanwhile Bonnie is not happy when some unwanted attendees turn up at her dance class. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben is disappointed when he misses Emma's first steps so Bonnie, Tucker and Riley try to recreate the moment for him. However, the activity causes the group to contemplate how much their lives have changed since the child's arrival. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau(n)

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Vince tries to strike up a friendship with Mike by inviting him to a crucial basketball game, but Molly is not impressed by their new-found bond(n)

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Peggy asks Molly to look after her dog when she is admitted to hospital for gall bladder surgery, and Mike gives his girlfriend cause for concern when he starts to talk about where he would like to be buried when he dies. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry's favourite shirt is hated by both his mother Beverly and his girlfriend Lainey, so they hatch a plan together to destroy it. However, once the shirt goes missing, Barry asks Adam and Erica to investigate. Murray decides to tear down the family tree house, leaving Adam distraught(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam worries about being 'hazed' by the older students when he goes to high school, but he soon earns their respect by body-slamming Barry. Erica and her friends talk about what they are going to put into their school-time capsule(n)

programme.desc: When Jake's favourite pizzeria burns down, the fire marshal (Patton Oswalt) believes the owner should be the prime suspect in the investigation. Meanwhile, chaos ensues at the precinct after a computer virus publishes everyone's internet browser history(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Santiago hosts a Thanksgiving dinner at her apartment for all her colleagues, but the event risks turning into a disaster, especially when Holt and Peralta have to leave in a hurry to catch a thief who has stolen a large sum of money from the evidence lock-up. Can Boyle save the day?(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj feels upset about not being in a relationship, so Penny sets out to find a date and introduces him to a girl he can actually talk to. They hit it off, but when it becomes apparent he has been buying her expensive gifts, his friends suspect she is only interested in his wealth(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon's old adversary Wil Wheaton returns and invites everyone to a house-warming party. Leonard is eager to go, but Sheldon threatens to shun him if he attends. However, he soon changes his mind when he finds out that Star Trek: The Next Generation actor Brent Spiner will also be there(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy take a train ride to the Napa Valley with Howard and Bernadette on Valentine's Day, while Leonard and Penny head to the vets when they volunteer to look after Raj's dog. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.desc: Amy and Sheldon start a gossip experiment to see how quickly rumours spread among the gang, while Howard tells his friends he is planning to propose to Bernadette - not realising Raj is wrestling with his own feelings for her. programme.desc: Sheldon is infuriated when Priya uses her legal knowledge to find a loophole in the room-mate agreement - but his spirits are lifted by a night on the town with Amy, Penny and Bernadette. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Johnny Galecki and Kaley Cuoco(n)

programme.desc: Adam works on a new persona that he hopes will make him seem cool on his first day at high school. Barry decides he wants to be a gym teacher and starts to act as Coach Mellor's apprentice. David Koechner and Allie Grant guest star(n)

programme.desc: Peralta tries to make a deal with a suspect Diaz has just arrested in an attempt to catch a car thief he has been tracking for years. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon's mother comes to visit and woos the gang, but he cannot help feeling jealous of all the attention she is receiving - until a bout of illness lays him low, and he suddenly sees the benefits of having her around. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons(n)

programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin help a couple from Australia looking for a holiday home within reach of their daughter in England, viewing properties in West Yorkshire and Normandy(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Derek and Julie Kalinski want to buy a holiday home where they can enjoy their retirement together. However, Julia wants to buy a property closer to home on the north Devon coast, while keen sailor Derek dreams of owning a French property in Brittany. Using a budget of £400,000 Jasmine Harman goes all out flying the flag for north Devon, while Jonnie Irwin tempts them with some stunning properties in France(n)

programme.desc: Air ambulance crew members take drastic steps to reset a patient's dangerously fast heartbeat, a cyclist crashes in a remote area, and a motorist is injured in a road accident(n)
